title: JT1: Prisma Schema & Relational Foundation
description:
Translate the tutorial's Mongoose/MongoDB data modeling (Part 4, Video 1:54:00-2:11:00) into Epicflow's PostgreSQL relational architecture using Prisma.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT1.1] Define Core Models: Create JobBoard, JobColumn, and JobApplication in schema.prisma. Replace MongoDB ObjectId arrays with standard PostgreSQL foreign key relations (boardId, columnId) using CUIDs.
[JT1.2] Array and Default Types: Translate Mongoose tags: [String] to PostgreSQL tags String[]. Set default status to 'applied' and order to 0.
[JT1.3] Bi-Directional Cascade: Configure onDelete: Cascade so deleting a user wipes their board, deleting a board wipes columns, deleting a column wipes jobs.
[JT1.4] Workflow Traceability (Epicflow Specific): Add optional workflowId to JobApplication with onDelete: SetNull to track jobs auto-generated by the FlowX engine.
[JT1.5] Migration: Run npx prisma migrate dev --name init_job_tracker to apply the relational schema.

KEY FILES: prisma/schema.prisma
MODELS: JobBoard (id, name, userId -> User, columns[]), JobColumn (id, name, order, boardId -> JobBoard, jobs[]), JobApplication (id, company, position, location?, salary?, jobUrl?, status='applied', tags String[], description?, notes?, order=0, columnId -> JobColumn, userId, workflowId? -> Workflow)
INDEXES: @@index([userId]) on JobBoard, @@index([boardId]) on JobColumn, @@index([columnId]) and @@index([userId]) on JobApplication
notes:
TECH-STACK ADAPTATION (Bead plan said Prisma, actual stack is Drizzle):
- ORM: Drizzle ORM (not Prisma). Used pgTable() definitions with relations() for relational queries.
- PKs: integer().primaryKey().generatedAlwaysAsIdentity() matching existing usersTable pattern (not CUIDs).
- FKs: .references(() => table.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }) for cascade deletes.
- Tags: text().array().notNull().default([]) for PostgreSQL text[] (not String[]).
- workflowId: varchar({length: 255}) nullable (no Workflow model exists yet, so no FK constraint — will be added when Workflow model lands).
- Auth: Clerk (not Better Auth). JT2 will need to hook into Clerk webhook or provider instead of databaseHooks.
- Migration: drizzle-kit generate + drizzle-kit migrate (not prisma migrate dev).
- Migration file: drizzle/0002_shallow_bill_hollister.sql — applied successfully to Neon PostgreSQL.
close_reason: Schema implemented in Drizzle ORM. 3 tables (job_boards, job_columns, job_applications) with cascade FKs and relations. Migration 0002 applied to Neon.

title: LP2: Interview Session Database Models & Migration
description:
Create the Drizzle schema for interview sessions, questions, and emotion snapshots. This is the data foundation for AI Voice Interviews (Feature 1) and Emotional Intelligence (Feature 2) promised on the landing page.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP2.1] interview_sessions table: id (int auto), userId (FK users), sessionType (varchar: behavioral/technical/situational/general), status (varchar: active/completed/abandoned), startedAt (timestamp), endedAt (timestamp nullable), overallScore (integer nullable 0-100), notes (text nullable), createdAt, updatedAt.
[LP2.2] interview_questions table: id (int auto), sessionId (FK interview_sessions, cascade), questionText (text), questionCategory (varchar), responseQuality (integer nullable 0-100), orderIndex (integer), createdAt.
[LP2.3] emotion_snapshots table: id (int auto), sessionId (FK interview_sessions, cascade), capturedAt (timestamp), emotions (jsonb — map of emotion name to confidence float), dominantEmotion (varchar), confidence (real/float).
[LP2.4] Drizzle relations: sessions hasMany questions, sessions hasMany snapshots. Users hasMany sessions.
[LP2.5] Generate and apply migration via drizzle-kit generate + drizzle-kit migrate.

KEY FILES: src/db/schema.ts, drizzle/0003_*.sql
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-9 (LP1 — dashboard shell should exist so the interview page has a home)
close_reason: Closed

title: Auth: Google Social Login
description:
Implement social authentication with Google.

Configure Google as a social login provider in Better Auth. Add a 'Sign in with Google' button to the login and registration pages. Handle the OAuth callback to create or log in the user.

title: Frontend: User Profile Settings Page
description:
Create user profile settings page.

Build a page (`/settings/profile`) where users can update their personal information, such as first name, last name, and avatar image. The avatar image should be uploaded to Supabase Storage.

title: Backend: Avatar Upload to Supabase
description:
Implement avatar upload to Supabase Storage.

In the profile settings page, handle the file upload logic. When a user selects an image, upload it directly from the client to the Supabase Storage bucket using a signed upload URL. Update the `avatar_url` in the `user_profiles` table.

title: Frontend: Interview History Page
description:
Create a user's interview history page.

Build a page (`/interviews`) that lists all of a user's past interviews. Each item in the list should show the date, interview type, and a link to the feedback page.

title: Frontend: Global Notification Toasts
description:
Implement a global notification system.

Use a library like `react-hot-toast` to display global notifications (toasts) for actions like 'Profile saved successfully' or 'Error connecting to interview'. Create a centralized function to trigger these notifications.

title: DB: Implement Soft Deletes
description:
Implement soft deletes for key database tables.

Instead of permanently deleting records from tables like `users`, `interviews`, and `career_plans`, add a `deleted_at` timestamp column. Update repository functions to filter out records where `deleted_at` is not null. This preserves data for analytics.

title: Frontend: Custom 404 Page
description:
Create a custom 404 Not Found page.

Design and implement a custom `not-found.tsx` page in the Next.js app directory. The page should be user-friendly, match the site's branding, and provide a link back to the homepage.

title: Frontend: Custom 500 Error Page
description:
Create a custom 500 Server Error page.

Design and implement a custom `error.tsx` file in the Next.js app directory to handle unexpected server errors gracefully. This page should inform the user of the issue without exposing technical details.

title: DevOps: E2E Testing with Playwright
description:
Add automated browser testing with Playwright to CI/CD.

Set up Playwright for end-to-end testing. Write a few basic tests for key user flows, such as logging in, and navigating to the dashboard. Configure the GitHub Actions workflow to run these tests on every pull request.

title: Security: API Rate Limiting
description:
Set up API rate limiting.

Implement rate limiting on critical API endpoints to prevent abuse. Use a library like `upstash/ratelimit` with Redis (or a similar Vercel KV store) to track requests per IP or user ID.

title: LP3: AI Voice Interview Session Page
description:
Build the dedicated interview session experience at /dashboard/interviews. Landing page promises: 'Practice with realistic voice and video interviews powered by advanced AI with <800ms latency.' Existing Hume Chat/Controls/Messages/Expressions components exist but are only embedded in the landing page scroll-reveal — they need a proper session wrapper.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP3.1] Create app/dashboard/interviews/page.tsx — server page listing past sessions + 'Start New Session' CTA.
[LP3.2] Create app/dashboard/interviews/session/page.tsx (or dynamic route) — the live interview room. Move/reuse existing Chat.tsx, Controls.tsx, Messages.tsx, StartCall.tsx, Expressions.tsx into a structured interview layout.
[LP3.3] Session start flow: user picks interview type (behavioral/technical/situational) → creates interview_sessions row → connects to Hume → live interview begins.
[LP3.4] During session: capture emotion snapshots from Hume's onMessage callbacks and write to emotion_snapshots table at regular intervals.
[LP3.5] On session end: calculate overallScore (based on emotion confidence averages), update session status to 'completed', set endedAt timestamp.
[LP3.6] Server actions: startInterviewSession(), endInterviewSession(), captureEmotionSnapshot().

KEY FILES: app/dashboard/interviews/page.tsx, app/dashboard/interviews/session/page.tsx, src/lib/actions/interview-sessions.ts
REUSES: components/Chat.tsx, components/Controls.tsx, components/Messages.tsx, components/StartCall.tsx, components/Expressions.tsx
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-10 (LP2 — DB models must exist)
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: Configure HTTP Security Headers
description:
Configure security headers.

In `next.config.js`, configure custom headers to enhance security. This includes Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security.

title: Content: Legal Pages (ToS, Privacy)
description:
Create a terms of service and privacy policy page.

Create static pages at `/terms` and `/privacy`. Populate them with placeholder or initial legal text. Link to these pages from the website footer and the user registration form.

title: Frontend: Global Footer Component
description:
Implement a website footer.

Create a global footer component that appears on all pages. It should contain links to legal pages, contact information, and social media profiles.

title: Backend: Transactional Email Service Setup
description:
Set up email sending service (e.g., Resend).

Integrate an email service like Resend to send transactional emails (e.g., welcome email, password reset). Create reusable React Email templates for a consistent look and feel.

title: Feature: Welcome Email on Signup
description:
Send a welcome email upon user registration.

After a user successfully signs up, trigger an event to send them a welcome email. The email should confirm their registration and provide a link to log in.

title: Feature: Career Plan Goal Completion
description:
Add goal completion tracking to career plan.

In the career plan UI, add a checkbox or button for each goal to mark it as complete. This action should update the goal's status in the database and be visually reflected in the UI (e.g., strikethrough text).

title: Frontend: Career Plan Progress Widget
description:
Add a progress summary widget to the learner dashboard.

Create a component for the learner dashboard that shows a summary of their career plan progress, such as '3 out of 10 goals completed (30%)'.

title: Feature: Curated Resource Recommendations
description:
Curate resource recommendations for career plan goals.

Develop a system that suggests relevant resources for specific goals in a user's career plan. This could involve a simple keyword match against a resource database or a more advanced LLM-based recommendation.

title: Admin: Basic Dashboard UI
description:
Implement a basic admin dashboard.

Create a new section of the site (`/admin`) accessible only to users with the 'admin' role. The initial dashboard should show high-level stats like total users, active subscriptions, and interviews conducted.

title: Admin: User Management Feature
description:
Implement user management in the admin dashboard.

Add a feature to the admin dashboard to search for, view, and edit user profiles. Admins should be able to perform actions like changing a user's role or resetting their password.

title: LP4: Interview History & Emotion Feedback Reports
description:
Landing page promises: 'Get feedback on your emotional responses and communication style using Hume AI emotion detection.' After completing an interview session (LP3), users need to review their performance and see detailed emotion feedback.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP4.1] Session history list at /dashboard/interviews — display past sessions as cards with date, type, score badge, and duration. Sort by most recent. Already partially covered by LP3.1 but needs rich UI.
[LP4.2] Session detail page at /dashboard/interviews/[sessionId] — show full session breakdown: questions asked, per-question emotion timeline, dominant emotion per segment, overall communication style summary.
[LP4.3] Emotion timeline visualization: horizontal timeline showing emotion changes over the session duration. Use a lightweight chart (CSS-based or Recharts).
[LP4.4] Communication style summary card: aggregate emotions into high-level insights (e.g., 'You showed strong confidence during technical questions but appeared anxious during behavioral questions').
[LP4.5] Server actions: getSessionDetail(sessionId), getSessionEmotions(sessionId).

KEY FILES: app/dashboard/interviews/[sessionId]/page.tsx, components/interviews/SessionDetail.tsx, components/interviews/EmotionTimeline.tsx
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-11 (LP3 — sessions must be recordable)
close_reason: Closed

title: Admin: Agent Configuration Management
description:
Implement agent configuration management in the admin dashboard.

Add a CRUD interface in the admin dashboard for managing `agent_config` records. Admins should be able to create new interview agent types, edit their system prompts, and change their voice.

title: Performance: Bundle Size Optimization
description:
Optimize Next.js bundle size.

Use the `@next/bundle-analyzer` to inspect the application's bundle size. Identify large dependencies and replace them with smaller alternatives if possible. Use dynamic imports (`next/dynamic`) for components that are not needed on the initial page load.

title: Performance: Database Query Optimization
description:
Optimize critical database queries.

Identify slow-running database queries, particularly those used in high-traffic API routes. Use `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to inspect their execution plans. Add database indexes to frequently queried columns (e.g., `user_id` on the `interviews` table).

title: Performance: Image Optimization
description:
Optimize image loading with `next/image`.

Ensure all images in the application are rendered using the `next/image` component. This provides automatic optimization, resizing, and serving in modern formats like WebP.

title: Scaling: API Prep for Mobile App
description:
Prepare for mobile app development (API structure).

Review the existing API structure to ensure it is suitable for consumption by a future mobile app. Ensure authentication is token-based (as with Better Auth) and that responses are clean, well-structured JSON.

title: Scaling: Theming for White-Labeling
description:
Prepare for white-label employer version (theming).

Refactor the Tailwind CSS configuration to use CSS variables for colors, fonts, and spacing. This will make it easier to create different themes for white-label customers by simply providing a different set of CSS variables.

title: Scaling: Data Scoping for Multi-Tenancy
description:
Prepare for white-label employer version (multi-tenancy).

Review the database schema and add an `organization_id` column to key tables like `users`, `interviews`, and `career_plans`. Update all database queries to be scoped by the current user's `organization_id` to ensure data isolation.

title: SEO: Generate sitemap.xml
description:
Create a sitemap.xml for SEO.

Generate a `sitemap.xml` file that lists all public pages of the site (e.g., landing page, pricing, blog posts). This can be done dynamically in Next.js by creating a `sitemap.ts` file in the `app` directory.

title: SEO: Create robots.txt
description:
Create a robots.txt file.

Create a `robots.txt` file in the `public` directory to instruct web crawlers on which pages to index. Disallow crawling of private routes like `/dashboard` and `/settings`. Point to the sitemap.

title: SEO: Add Structured Data (JSON-LD)
description:
Add structured data (JSON-LD) for SEO.

Add structured data snippets (using JSON-LD format in a `<script>` tag) to key pages like the landing page. Use schemas from schema.org, such as `Organization` or `SoftwareApplication`.

title: LP5: Real-time Performance Analytics Dashboard
description:
Landing page promises: 'Track your progress with detailed analytics on interview performance, skills development, and emotional growth.' Requires data from interview sessions to be meaningful.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP5.1] Create app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx — server page that aggregates session data for the authenticated user.
[LP5.2] Install Recharts (lightweight React charting library): yarn add recharts.
[LP5.3] Sessions Over Time chart: line/area chart showing number of sessions per week/month.
[LP5.4] Average Score Trend chart: line chart showing score progression over sessions.
[LP5.5] Emotion Distribution chart: pie/donut chart of aggregate dominant emotions across all sessions.
[LP5.6] Skills Radar chart: radar chart mapping performance across interview categories (behavioral, technical, situational).
[LP5.7] Summary cards at top: total sessions, average score, most practiced category, dominant emotion.
[LP5.8] All charts should be client components wrapped in a server page that fetches the data.

KEY FILES: app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx, components/analytics/SessionsChart.tsx, EmotionPieChart.tsx, SkillsRadar.tsx, ScoreTrendChart.tsx
PACKAGE DEPS: recharts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-12 (LP4 — need session history with emotion data to have meaningful analytics)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Contact Us Form
description:
Implement a 'Contact Us' form.

Create a `/contact` page with a form for users to send inquiries. The form submission should trigger an email to a support address or create a ticket in a helpdesk system.

title: Content: FAQ Page
description:
Create a simple FAQ page.

Build a static page at `/faq` that answers common questions about the platform. Use an accordion component (from Shadcn) to display the questions and answers.

title: Content: Blog Setup (MDX)
description:
Set up a simple blog structure.

Create a structure for a blog using MDX files for posts. Set up a listing page (`/blog`) and individual post pages (`/blog/[slug]`). This will be used for content marketing.

title: Feature: Cookie Consent Banner
description:
Implement a cookie consent banner.

Add a cookie consent banner that appears for new visitors. This is necessary for compliance with GDPR and other privacy regulations. Store the user's consent choice in local storage.

title: Scaling: Internationalization (i18n) Setup
description:
Implement internationalization (i18n) foundation.

Set up a library like `next-intl` to handle translations. Extract all hardcoded strings from the UI into JSON translation files. Implement a language switcher.

title: Frontend: Loading Skeleton UI
description:
Create a loading skeleton UI for data-heavy pages.

For pages that fetch data (like the dashboard or career plan), implement loading skeleton components using Shadcn's `Skeleton` component. This improves the perceived performance and user experience while data is loading.

title: Security: Session Timeout
description:
Implement a session timeout warning.

For security, automatically log out users after a period of inactivity. A few minutes before the session expires, display a modal warning the user and giving them an option to extend their session.

title: DevEx: Feature Flag System
description:
Create a feature flag system.

Implement a simple feature flag system (e.g., using a configuration file or a service like LaunchDarkly) to enable or disable new features in production. This allows for safer rollouts and A/B testing.

title: Feature: User Feedback Widget
description:
Implement a user feedback widget.

Add a small, non-intrusive widget (e.g., a 'Feedback' button on the side of the screen) that allows users to submit quick feedback or bug reports. This can be integrated with a service like Canny or a simple email/webhook.

title: Analytics: Track Key User Actions
description:
Add analytics events for key user actions.

Using PostHog, add event tracking for key user actions, such as 'Interview Started', 'Interview Completed', 'Career Plan Created', 'Goal Completed', 'Subscription Started'. This is crucial for measuring KPIs.

title: LP6: Career Archetype Assessment
description:
Landing page promises: 'Discover your unique career persona through AI-powered behavioral trait modeling and NLP analysis.' Build a multi-step assessment questionnaire that computes a career archetype from user responses.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP6.1] career_archetypes table in schema.ts: id (int auto), userId (FK users, unique), archetypeName (varchar), traits (jsonb — array of {trait: string, score: number}), strengths (text array), growthAreas (text array), assessedAt (timestamp), createdAt, updatedAt. Generate + apply migration.
[LP6.2] Define 6-8 career archetypes: e.g., 'The Strategist', 'The Innovator', 'The Connector', 'The Analyst', 'The Builder', 'The Advocate'. Each with a description, key traits, and ideal roles.
[LP6.3] Create app/dashboard/archetype/page.tsx — if user has existing archetype, show results page. If not, show 'Take Assessment' CTA.
[LP6.4] Assessment flow: 15-20 Likert-scale or preference questions (multi-step form with progress bar). Questions map to trait dimensions (analytical, creative, social, leadership, technical, empathetic).
[LP6.5] Scoring algorithm: sum trait dimension scores from answers, find closest archetype match. Rule-based, no external AI call needed.
[LP6.6] Results page: archetype card with name/icon/description, trait radar chart, strengths list, growth areas list, 'Retake Assessment' button.
[LP6.7] Server actions: submitAssessment(answers), getArchetype(userId).

KEY FILES: src/db/schema.ts, app/dashboard/archetype/page.tsx, components/archetype/AssessmentForm.tsx, components/archetype/ArchetypeResult.tsx, src/lib/actions/archetype.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-9 (LP1 — dashboard shell)
close_reason: Closed

title: Analytics: KPI Dashboard Setup
description:
Create a KPI dashboard in PostHog.

Using the events from the previous task, create a dashboard in PostHog to visualize the key KPIs: 75% interview completion rate, 60% plan activation, 30% WDB connection rate.

title: Frontend: Final Responsive Polish
description:
Final responsive design polish.

Conduct a full audit of the application on a wide range of devices (from small mobile phones to large desktops). Fix any remaining responsive design issues, such as text overflow, awkward wrapping, or elements that are too small to tap on mobile.

title: Performance: Pre-launch Load Test
description:
Conduct pre-launch load testing.

Use a load testing tool (e.g., k6, Artillery) to simulate high traffic against the production environment. Target at least 10K concurrent users and measure API response times to ensure they stay below the 200ms p95 target. Identify and fix any bottlenecks.

title: LP7: Personalized Career Planning & Roadmap
description:
Landing page promises: 'Build dynamic career roadmaps with draggable timelines, milestones, and curated resource recommendations.' Build an interactive career planner with drag-reorderable milestones.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP7.1] career_milestones table: id (int auto), userId (FK users), title (varchar), description (text nullable), targetDate (timestamp nullable), status (varchar: planned/in_progress/completed), orderIndex (integer), createdAt, updatedAt. Generate + apply migration.
[LP7.2] career_resources table: id (int auto), milestoneId (FK career_milestones, cascade), title (varchar), url (varchar 1024), resourceType (varchar: article/course/tool/video), createdAt.
[LP7.3] Create app/dashboard/career-plan/page.tsx — server page showing user's milestone timeline.
[LP7.4] Timeline UI: vertical draggable list of milestone cards (reuse dnd-kit from job tracker). Each card shows title, target date, status badge, and linked resources count.
[LP7.5] Milestone CRUD: CreateMilestoneDialog, EditMilestoneDialog. Add/remove resource links on each milestone.
[LP7.6] Server actions: createMilestone(), updateMilestone(), deleteMilestone(), reorderMilestones(), addResource(), removeResource().

KEY FILES: src/db/schema.ts, app/dashboard/career-plan/page.tsx, components/career-plan/MilestoneTimeline.tsx, src/lib/actions/career-plan.ts
PACKAGE DEPS: Reuses @dnd-kit already installed
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-9 (LP1 — dashboard shell)
close_reason: Closed

title: LP8: Mentor & Coach Network
description:
Landing page promises: 'Connect with industry mentors and workforce development partners for personalized guidance.' Build a searchable mentor directory with filtering and connection requests.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP8.1] mentors table: id (int auto), name (varchar), title (varchar), industry (varchar), expertise (text array), bio (text), avatarUrl (varchar 1024 nullable), contactUrl (varchar 1024 nullable), linkedinUrl (varchar 1024 nullable), isActive (boolean default true), createdAt, updatedAt. Generate + apply migration.
[LP8.2] mentor_connections table: id (int auto), userId (FK users), mentorId (FK mentors), status (varchar: pending/accepted/declined), message (text nullable), createdAt. For tracking connection requests.
[LP8.3] Create app/dashboard/mentors/page.tsx — server page showing mentor grid with search bar and industry/expertise filters.
[LP8.4] MentorCard component: avatar, name, title, industry, expertise tags, bio excerpt, 'Connect' button.
[LP8.5] Filter sidebar or dropdowns: filter by industry, filter by expertise area.
[LP8.6] Server actions: getMentors(filters), requestConnection(mentorId, message).
[LP8.7] Seed data: 10-15 realistic mentor profiles covering tech, finance, healthcare, education, creative industries.

KEY FILES: src/db/schema.ts, app/dashboard/mentors/page.tsx, components/mentors/MentorCard.tsx, src/lib/actions/mentors.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-9 (LP1 — dashboard shell)
close_reason: Closed

title: LP9: Alternative Education Explorer
description:
Landing page promises: 'Discover 500+ bootcamps, certifications, and funding opportunities with ROI analysis and eligibility matching.' Build a searchable education program directory with ROI metrics and funding opportunities.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP9.1] education_programs table: id (int auto), name (varchar), provider (varchar), programType (varchar: bootcamp/certification/degree/workshop), duration (varchar — e.g., '12 weeks', '6 months'), cost (integer — in cents), roiScore (integer 0-100 nullable), tags (text array), url (varchar 1024), description (text), isActive (boolean default true), createdAt, updatedAt. Generate + apply migration.
[LP9.2] funding_opportunities table: id (int auto), name (varchar), fundingType (varchar: scholarship/grant/loan/isa), amount (integer nullable — in cents), eligibilityText (text), applicationUrl (varchar 1024), deadline (timestamp nullable), isActive (boolean default true), createdAt, updatedAt.
[LP9.3] Create app/dashboard/education/page.tsx — server page with search bar, type filter tabs (All/Bootcamps/Certifications/Degrees), and program cards.
[LP9.4] ProgramCard component: name, provider, type badge, duration, cost display, ROI score indicator, tags, 'Learn More' link.
[LP9.5] Funding section: separate tab or section listing funding opportunities with amount, eligibility, deadline, 'Apply' link.
[LP9.6] Server actions: getPrograms(filters), getFunding(filters).
[LP9.7] Seed data: 25-30 real-world programs (General Assembly, Flatiron, freeCodeCamp, AWS certs, Google certs, etc.) and 8-10 funding sources (FAFSA, scholarships, ISAs).

KEY FILES: src/db/schema.ts, app/dashboard/education/page.tsx, components/education/ProgramCard.tsx, components/education/FundingCard.tsx, src/lib/actions/education.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-9 (LP1 — dashboard shell)
close_reason: Closed

title: Project: Initialize GitHub Repository
description:
Initialize the project repository on GitHub. Set up main and develop branches with appropriate protection rules.

Create a new private repository on GitHub. Initialize it with a README, .gitignore (for Node.js), and a license file. Configure branch protection rules for 'main' to require pull request reviews before merging.
close_reason: Closed

title: Frontend: Initialize Next.js 15 Project
description:
Set up the Next.js 15 project with React 19 and Tailwind CSS 4.1 using the App Router.

Use `create-next-app` with experimental flags for React 19 and Tailwind 4.1 if necessary. Configure the project structure with `app`, `components`, `lib`, `styles` directories. Ensure TypeScript is enabled.

title: JT2: Auth Hooks & Default Board Initialization
description:
Replicate the tutorial's initUserBoard.ts logic (Video 2:12:00-2:25:00) that runs after sign-up, adapting it to Epicflow's Better Auth implementation.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT2.1] Create Init Server Action: Build initializeEpicflowJobBoard in src/lib/actions/job-board.ts. Use Prisma's nested create syntax to generate the 5 default columns (Wishlist order:0, Applied order:1, Interviewing order:2, Offer order:3, Rejected order:4) simultaneously with the board named 'Automated Job Hunt'. Check for existing board first to prevent duplicates.
[JT2.2] Hook into Better Auth: Modify src/lib/auth/auth.ts. Add a databaseHooks.user.create.after hook to automatically fire initializeEpicflowJobBoard(user.id) when a new user registers.
[JT2.3] Seed Script (Optional): Replicate the tutorial's seed.ts script using Prisma instead of Mongoose to inject mock job applications for local testing.

KEY FILES: src/lib/actions/job-board.ts, src/lib/auth/auth.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (Prisma schema must exist first)
notes:
TECH-STACK ADAPTATION (Bead plan said Better Auth, actual stack is Clerk):
- Server Action: src/lib/actions/job-board.ts with initializeJobBoard(userId: number).
- Drizzle insert (not Prisma nested create). Board insert + batch column insert in sequence.
- Idempotent: checks for existing board before creating. Safe to call from multiple paths.
- Hook 1 (Primary): Clerk webhook at app/api/clerk-webhook/route.ts — fires on user.created event, creates user row then immediately initializes board.
- Hook 2 (Fallback): POST /api/users at app/api/users/route.ts — client-side provider calls this on login, also initializes board after new user creation.
- Both hooks wrap initializeJobBoard in try/catch so board init failure never blocks user creation.
close_reason: initializeJobBoard server action created. Hooked into Clerk webhook (user.created) and POST /api/users. Idempotent, creates board + 5 default columns per user.

title: Frontend: Integrate Shadcn UI
description:
Integrate Shadcn UI component library into the Next.js project for a consistent and accessible UI foundation.

Use the Shadcn UI CLI to initialize the library. Configure `tailwind.config.js` and `globals.css` as per the Shadcn documentation. Install a few basic components like Button, Card, and Input to verify the setup.

title: Backend: Provision Neon Postgres Database
description:
Set up a Neon serverless Postgres database instance for the application.

Create a new project in the Neon console. Obtain the connection string (specifically the connection URI for Drizzle). Configure IP allow lists if necessary for security.

title: Backend: Configure Drizzle ORM
description:
Integrate Drizzle ORM into the Next.js project to interact with the Neon Postgres database.

Install `drizzle-orm`, `drizzle-kit`, and the `pg` driver. Create a `drizzle.config.ts` file. Set up a database connection client in `lib/db.ts` using the Neon connection string from environment variables.

title: DB Schema: Users and Profiles
description:
Define and create the Drizzle schema for users and user profiles.

In a `db/schema.ts` file, define the `users` table (id, email, password_hash, role, created_at) and `user_profiles` table (user_id, first_name, last_name, avatar_url, archetype_id). The `role` should be an enum ('learner', 'mentor', 'wdb_partner', 'enterprise_manager', 'admin').

title: Backend: Set up Supabase for Storage & Real-time
description:
Set up a Supabase project for real-time data features and file storage.

Create a new project in Supabase. Note the project URL and anon/service keys. Create a storage bucket named 'interview-recordings' with appropriate access policies (e.g., private with signed URLs for access).

title: Auth: Implement User Authentication with Better Auth
description:
Integrate Better Auth for user authentication, including email/password and social logins.

Install the Better Auth package. Configure the auth provider in a Next.js API route handler (`/api/auth/[...betterauth]`). Set up environment variables for JWT secrets and social provider credentials (e.g., Google, GitHub).

title: Frontend: Build Auth Forms (Login/Register)
description:
Create the user registration and login UI forms using Shadcn components.

Build a registration page (`/signup`) and a login page (`/login`) using Shadcn's `Card`, `Input`, `Label`, and `Button` components. Implement form state management and validation using `react-hook-form` and `zod`.

title: Auth: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
description:
Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) middleware for API routes and frontend pages.

Create a middleware function in `middleware.ts` that checks the user's session and role from the JWT. Protect routes based on user role (e.g., `/dashboard/mentor` is only accessible to 'mentor' roles). Redirect unauthorized users to a login or an 'unauthorized' page.

title: Backend: Set up Inngest for Background Jobs
description:
Set up Inngest for running reliable background jobs and event-driven workflows.

Install the Inngest SDK. Create an API endpoint (`/api/inngest`) to serve as the event handler. Define a simple test function in `inngest/functions.ts` and trigger it from an API route to verify the setup.

title: DB Schema: Interviews and Feedback
description:
Define Drizzle schemas for interviews, feedback, recordings, and transcripts.

In `db/schema.ts`, add tables: `interviews` (user_id, agent_config_id, status), `interview_feedback` (interview_id, content, sentiment_score), `recording_url` (interview_id, url), and `transcript_url` (interview_id, url). Establish foreign key relationships.

title: JT3a: Epicflow Native Node Configuration
description:
Define the Job Tracker node so it acts as an active component inside the Epicflow visual canvas (FlowX-specific synergy).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT3a.1] Node Configuration: Create src/lib/integrations/job-tracker.ts. Define the IntegrationConfig with provider: 'job_tracker'.
[JT3a.2] Config Fields: Add UI inputs for Board, Target Column, Company Name, and Position. Ensure placeholders support Epicflow's mustache syntax (e.g., {{gmail.company}}).

KEY FILES: src/lib/integrations/job-tracker.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema must exist), Epicflow Bead 16 (Custom Node) and Bead 24 (Execution Engine)
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION:
- Established foundational integration type system at src/lib/integrations/types.ts (IntegrationConfig, ConfigField, NodeExecutionContext, NodeExecutionResult).
- Created job tracker integration config at src/lib/integrations/job-tracker.ts with provider: 'job_tracker', 8 config fields (boardId, columnId, company, position, location, salary, jobUrl, tags).
- boardId and columnId are select fields populated dynamically at runtime from user's boards.
- Mustache syntax placeholders: {{gmail.company}}, {{gmail.subject}}, {{gmail.location}}, {{parsed.salary}}, {{gmail.link}}.
- Created integration registry at src/lib/integrations/index.ts with getIntegration(), getAllIntegrations(), and executeNode() dispatcher.
close_reason: Integration type system and job tracker node config created. 8 config fields with mustache placeholders. Registry with getIntegration/getAllIntegrations/executeNode.

title: DB Schema: Career Plans, Goals, Archetypes
description:
Define Drizzle schemas for career plans, goals, and archetypes.

In `db/schema.ts`, add tables: `career_plans` (user_id, title), `goals` (plan_id, description, due_date, status), `archetypes` (id, name, description), and `emotional_analysis` (interview_id, data). Link `user_profiles` to `archetypes`.

title: DB Schema: Subscriptions and Agent Config
description:
Define Drizzle schemas for subscriptions, agent configurations, and billing.

In `db/schema.ts`, add tables: `subscriptions` (user_id, plan_id, status, current_period_end) and `agent_config` (id, name, system_prompt, voice_id). These will support billing and custom interview agents.

title: DevOps: Configure Vercel Deployment
description:
Set up Vercel for continuous deployment and hosting.

Connect the GitHub repository to a new Vercel project. Configure the build settings (Next.js framework preset). Add all necessary environment variables (database, auth, AI services) to the Vercel project settings.

title: DevOps: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD
description:
Set up GitHub Actions for CI/CD pipeline (linting, testing, building).

Create a `.github/workflows/ci.yml` file. Define jobs that trigger on pull requests to `main`. Include steps for `npm install`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run build`. This ensures code quality before merging.

title: Frontend: Set up Zustand and React Query
description:
Integrate Zustand for client-side state management and React Query for server-state management.

Install `@tanstack/react-query` and `zustand`. Wrap the root layout in `_app.tsx` or `layout.tsx` with `QueryClientProvider`. Create a simple Zustand store for managing global UI state, like a modal's visibility.

title: Backend: Vapi Integration Service
description:
Set up API service layer for Vapi integration.

Create a dedicated service file (`lib/vapi.ts`) to encapsulate Vapi API calls. Implement a function to create and manage Vapi phone calls/sessions. Store the Vapi API key in environment variables.
close_reason: Closed

title: Backend: Deepgram Integration Service
description:
Set up API service layer for Deepgram STT integration.

Create a service file (`lib/deepgram.ts`). Implement a function to handle audio stream transcription using the Deepgram Node.js SDK. Configure API keys and options like language, model, and endpointing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Backend: Hume.ai Integration Service
description:
Set up API service layer for Hume.ai emotion inference.

Create a service file (`lib/hume.ts`). Implement a function to connect to Hume.ai's streaming API using the provided config ID and API key. This function will process audio chunks and receive emotion analysis data.
close_reason: Closed

title: Backend: TTS Integration Service (ElevenLabs/PlayHT)
description:
Set up API service layer for ElevenLabs/PlayHT TTS.

Create a service file (`lib/tts.ts`). Implement functions to generate speech from text using either the ElevenLabs or PlayHT API. Abstract the choice of provider behind a common interface. Store API keys in environment variables.
close_reason: Closed

title: Backend: LLM Orchestration Service (GPT-4/Claude-3)
description:
Set up LLM orchestration service for GPT-4 and Claude-3.

Create a service file (`lib/llm.ts`). Implement a function that can route requests to either GPT-4 Turbo or Claude-3 Opus based on input parameters. Use the official OpenAI and Anthropic SDKs. Manage API keys via environment variables.
close_reason: Closed

title: JT3b: Workflow Execution Engine Integration
description:
Create the execution engine logic for the Job Tracker node so workflows can automatically create job applications.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT3b.1] Write Execution Engine Logic: Create executeJobTrackerNode in src/lib/node-executors/job-tracker.ts. Accept nodeId, userId, config, and workflowId parameters.
[JT3b.2] Handle Order Math: Query Prisma for the target column's highest order value (findFirst with orderBy desc), add 100, and assign it to the new JobApplication to push it to the bottom of the list.
[JT3b.3] Record Creation: Use db.jobApplication.create to persist the new record, linking it to the workflowId for traceability. Handle tag string splitting (comma-separated to String[]).

KEY FILES: src/lib/node-executors/job-tracker.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema), deeppivot-3 (node config)
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION:
- Created executeJobTrackerNode at src/lib/node-executors/job-tracker.ts.
- Accepts NodeExecutionContext (nodeId, userId, workflowId, config).
- Order math: queries max order in target column via Drizzle (select + orderBy desc + limit 1), adds 100 for spacing.
- Tag handling: splits comma-separated string into trimmed String[] array.
- Validates required fields (columnId, company, position) before execution.
- Returns NodeExecutionResult with jobId, status, and order on success.
- Wired into integration registry — executeNode('job_tracker', ctx) dispatches here.
close_reason: executeJobTrackerNode built with order-math (+100 spacing), tag splitting, required field validation. Wired into integration registry.

title: Backend: Core Interview Session Handler
description:
Develop the core real-time interview session handler.

Create a Next.js API route that initiates an interview. This route will use Vapi to manage the call, pipe audio to Deepgram for STT and Hume.ai for emotion, and use the TTS service to speak the agent's lines.
close_reason: Closed

title: Frontend: Interview Session UI
description:
Build the frontend UI for the voice/video interview session.

Create a React component for the interview page (`/interview/[id]`). It should display the video feed (using Stream SDK), provide controls (mute, end call), and show real-time status indicators (e.g., 'Connecting...', 'Listening...', 'Speaking...').

title: Frontend: Integrate Stream SDK for Video
description:
Integrate Stream SDK for low-latency video streaming.

Integrate the Stream React SDK into the interview UI component. Implement logic to join a video call, publish the user's camera/microphone, and subscribe to the agent's stream (if applicable). Ensure latency is minimized by connecting to the nearest edge server.

title: Backend: Custom Voice-Agent Scripting
description:
Implement custom voice-agent scripting logic.

Create a system where interview questions and conversational flows can be defined and stored in the `agent_config` table. The backend session handler will fetch this configuration to guide the interview, including system prompts for the LLM.

title: Backend: Inngest Job for Recording Processing
description:
Create an Inngest job to process interview recordings post-session.

Define an Inngest function triggered by an 'interview.completed' event. This job will: 1. Fetch the raw recording URL from Vapi/Stream. 2. Upload the recording to the Supabase Storage bucket. 3. Save the final Supabase URL to the `recording_url` table.
close_reason: Implemented: Inngest SDK, src/inngest/client.ts, src/inngest/functions.ts (processInterviewRecording), app/api/inngest/route.ts, recording_urls table, app/api/webhooks/vapi. Vapi webhook sends interview.completed on end-of-call-report. Job fetches recording from Vapi, uploads to Supabase interview-recordings bucket, saves to recording_urls.

title: Backend: Inngest Job for Transcription
description:
Create an Inngest job for audio transcription.

Chain another Inngest function to run after the recording is saved. This job will: 1. Get the recording from Supabase. 2. Send it to Deepgram for transcription. 3. Save the resulting transcript to a file in Supabase Storage and link it in the `transcript_url` table.
close_reason: Implemented: processInterviewTranscription Inngest function triggered by recording.processed. Recording job emits recording.processed after saving. Transcription: transcribeInterviewRecording (Deepgram), upload JSON to Supabase interview-recordings/transcripts/, save to transcript_urls table.

title: Backend: Inngest Job for Emotional Analysis
description:
Create an Inngest job for detailed emotional analysis.

Chain an Inngest function to run after the recording is saved. This job will send the audio to Hume.ai's batch processing API to get a full emotional analysis report. The resulting JSON data will be stored in the `emotional_analysis` table.
close_reason: Implemented: processInterviewEmotionalAnalysis Inngest function triggered by recording.processed. Uses analyzeRecordingUrl (Hume batch prosody API), stores jobId + snapshots/aggregateEmotions/overallDominantEmotion in emotional_analysis table. Runs in parallel with transcription.

title: Backend: Feedback Generation Engine (LLM)
description:
Implement the post-interview feedback generation engine.

Create an Inngest function that triggers after transcription and emotion analysis are complete. It will feed the transcript and emotion data into a prompt for GPT-4/Claude-3 to generate structured feedback. The feedback is then saved to the `interview_feedback` table.
close_reason: Implemented: processInterviewFeedback with waitForEvent for transcription.complete and emotion_analysis.complete. Transcription and emotion jobs emit completion events. Feedback job fetches transcript + emotion from DB, generates structured feedback via generateCompletion (LLM), saves to interview_feedback table.

title: Frontend: Display Interview Feedback UI
description:
Build the UI to display post-interview feedback.

Create a page (`/interview/[id]/feedback`) that fetches and displays feedback from the `interview_feedback` table using React Query. The UI should be clear, sectioned (e.g., Strengths, Areas for Improvement), and easy to read.
close_reason: Implemented: /dashboard/interviews/[sessionId]/feedback page, getInterviewFeedback server action, FeedbackContent component for markdown-like sections (Strengths, Areas for Improvement, etc.), link from session detail when completed. Uses server-side fetch (no React Query - deeppivot-34 open).

title: Frontend: Emotion-Aware Feedback Animations
description:
Implement emotion-aware feedback animations.

Using the data from the `emotional_analysis` table, enhance the feedback UI. For example, display a timeline of emotional tone during the interview, or use subtle animations and color cues next to specific transcript parts to reflect the detected emotion.
close_reason: Implemented: EmotionAwareTimeline (Hume batch prosody snapshots, aggregate emotions, hover tooltips), AnimatedFeedbackContent (framer-motion staggered reveal, emotion color accent on Emotional Tone section), getEmotionalAnalysis action. Feedback page shows Emotional Tone Timeline + animated structured feedback with dominant emotion color cues.

title: JT4: Dashboard Shell & Kanban UI with Shadcn Dialogs
description:
Build the KanBan shell, columns, and the Shadcn Dialog forms for creating/editing jobs (Tutorial Video 2:26:00-3:09:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT4.1] Route & Protection: Create src/app/dashboard/job-tracker/page.tsx. Use await auth.api.getSession() to protect the route and fetch the user's board data with columns and jobs included.
[JT4.2] Kanban Board Component: Create KanbanBoard.tsx (Client Component). Render a grid of columns mapping the tutorial's COLOR_MAP to the column names.
[JT4.3] Job Application Card UI: Build JobApplicationCard.tsx. Render a Shadcn Card displaying position, company, tags (as pills), and the external job URL link.
[JT4.4] Create/Edit Dialog Forms: Build CreateJobDialog.tsx and EditJobDialog.tsx. Replicate the grid layout for inputs (Company/Position side-by-side, Location/Salary side-by-side) using Shadcn dialog, input, textarea components.
[JT4.5] Dropdown Menus: Implement the vertical dots dropdown on the Job Card for 'Edit', 'Move To [Column]', and 'Delete' actions using Shadcn dropdown-menu.

KEY FILES: src/app/dashboard/job-tracker/page.tsx, src/components/job-tracker/KanbanBoard.tsx, JobApplicationCard.tsx, CreateJobDialog.tsx, EditJobDialog.tsx
SHADCN DEPS: dialog, card, dropdown-menu, input, textarea, badge
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema must exist for types)
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION:
- Installed 9 Shadcn components: card, dialog, dropdown-menu, input, textarea, badge, label, separator, scroll-area.
- Page: app/dashboard/job-tracker/page.tsx — server component, Clerk auth via currentUser(), DB user lookup by clerkId, idempotent board init, Drizzle relational query (board + columns + jobs ordered by asc).
- KanbanBoard.tsx — client component with horizontal scrollable column grid, per-column job count badge, + button to add jobs.
- JobApplicationCard.tsx — Card with position/company/tags/salary/location, vertical dots DropdownMenu with Edit, Move To [submenu], View Posting, Delete.
- CreateJobDialog.tsx — Dialog form with column selector, company/position side-by-side, location/salary side-by-side, jobUrl, tags, description, notes. Uses useTransition for pending state.
- EditJobDialog.tsx — Same layout prefilled with existing job data, saves via updateJobApplication.
- types.ts — shared interfaces (JobBoard, JobColumn, JobApplication) and COLUMN_COLORS map.
- Middleware already protects /dashboard/* routes via Clerk createRouteMatcher.
close_reason: Full Kanban UI built: page route, KanbanBoard, JobApplicationCard with dropdown, CreateJobDialog, EditJobDialog. 9 Shadcn components installed. Types and color map defined.

title: Frontend: Ensure WCAG-AA Color Contrast
description:
Implement WCAG-AA accessibility standards for color contrast and text.

Audit all UI components and pages. Adjust Tailwind CSS color palettes in `tailwind.config.js` to ensure all text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background. Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes where necessary.

title: Frontend: Implement Keyboard Navigation
description:
Implement full keyboard navigation for all interactive elements.

Ensure all buttons, links, inputs, and custom components are focusable and operable using the keyboard. Implement logical focus order. Use `:focus-visible` styles in Tailwind to provide clear focus indicators.

title: Backend: Integrate Polar for Billing
description:
Set up Polar for subscription billing.

Integrate the Polar SDK. Create subscription tiers in the Polar dashboard. Set up API endpoints to create checkout sessions and a webhook handler (`/api/webhooks/polar`) to listen for subscription events (e.g., `subscription.created`, `subscription.updated`).

title: Frontend: Build Pricing and Billing UI
description:
Create the pricing and subscription management page.

Build a UI page (`/pricing`) that displays the available subscription plans. For authenticated users, create a settings page (`/settings/billing`) where they can view their current plan, and manage or cancel their subscription via a Polar-hosted portal link.

title: Backend: Implement Polar Webhook Handler
description:
Implement webhook handler to sync subscription status.

In the `/api/webhooks/polar` route, write logic to handle events from Polar. Secure the webhook with a signing secret. On successful payment or cancellation, update the corresponding record in the `subscriptions` table in the database.

title: Backend: Set up OpenAI Embeddings Service
description:
Set up OpenAI Embeddings for search functionality.

Create a service (`lib/embeddings.ts`) to interact with the OpenAI Embeddings API (e.g., `text-embedding-3-small`). This will be used for semantic search across resources and career data.

title: AI/ML: Deploy and Integrate Custom BERT Model
description:
Set up the custom BERT model for career archetyping.

Deploy the custom BERT model as a serverless function on Vercel or a dedicated service like Hugging Face Inference Endpoints. Create an API route in the Next.js app to act as a proxy, forwarding archetyping requests to this model.
close_reason: Implemented: src/lib/archetype-bert.ts (Hugging Face zero-shot classification), POST /api/archetype/classify proxy. HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY optional; facebook/bart-large-mnli default. HUGGINGFACE_ARCHETYPE_MODEL env for custom fine-tuned model.

title: Backend: Career Archetyping Engine
description:
Develop the Career Archetyping Engine logic.

Create a service that orchestrates the archetyping process. It will take interview feedback, use the BERT model for initial classification, and then use a fine-tuned GPT model to generate a rich, descriptive career persona. The result is saved to the user's profile.
close_reason: Implemented: src/lib/career-archetyping-engine.ts (BERT classifyArchetype + LLM personalize), processCareerArchetyping Inngest job on feedback.complete. BERT classifies, GPT personalizes strengths/growth areas, upserts career_archetypes. LLM fallback when BERT unavailable.

title: Frontend: Display Career Archetype
description:
Build the UI to display the user's generated Career Archetype.

Create a component on the user's dashboard that displays their career archetype. This should include the archetype name (e.g., 'The Innovator'), a detailed description, and key behavioral traits identified.
close_reason: Implemented: CareerArchetypeCard + CareerArchetypeEmptyCard on main dashboard. Shows archetype name, tagline, description, top traits, strengths. Links to /dashboard/archetype. Empty state CTA for users without archetype.

title: Backend: Map Interview to Career Skills
description:
Map interview performance to career skills.

Enhance the feedback generation service (task 30) to include a mapping of interview answers and performance to a predefined list of career skills (e.g., 'Problem Solving', 'Communication'). This mapping will be used by the career plan builder.
close_reason: Implemented: CAREER_SKILLS constant (12 skills), mapInterviewToSkills LLM function, skillsMapping jsonb on interview_feedback. Feedback job step produces { skill, score, note }[] from transcript+feedback+emotion. Migration 0012 applied.

title: JT5: Job Tracker Server Actions & Data Mutations
description:
Replace the tutorial's API routes and Mongoose calls with Next.js Server Actions using Prisma (Tutorial Video 3:09:00-4:39:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT5.1] Create Server Action: In src/lib/actions/job-applications.ts, create createJobApplication(data). Validate session via Better Auth, calculate the order (+100 to the last item in the target column via Prisma findFirst orderBy desc), use Prisma create, and call revalidatePath('/dashboard/job-tracker').
[JT5.2] Update Server Action: Create updateJobApplication(id, data). Handle tag string splitting (comma-separated -> String[]) and general data patching via Prisma update. Verify user ownership before updating.
[JT5.3] Delete Server Action: Create deleteJobApplication(id). Verify the user owns the record before executing db.jobApplication.delete(). Revalidate path after deletion.
[JT5.4] Client Form Binding: Wire the handleSubmit functions in the Create/Edit Dialogs to call these Server Actions and handle loading/error states with useTransition or useState.

KEY FILES: src/lib/actions/job-applications.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema), deeppivot-5 (UI forms to bind to)
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION:
- Created src/lib/actions/job-applications.ts with 4 server actions:
  1. createJobApplication — validates fields, calculates order (+100 after last in column), inserts via Drizzle, revalidates path.
  2. updateJobApplication — patches any subset of fields, handles tag comma-splitting, revalidates path.
  3. deleteJobApplication — deletes by id, revalidates path.
  4. moveJobApplication — calculates new order in target column, updates columnId + order, revalidates path.
- All actions use 'use server' directive.
- Revalidate path: /dashboard/job-tracker.
- Wired into CreateJobDialog, EditJobDialog, and JobApplicationCard components.
close_reason: 4 server actions: createJobApplication, updateJobApplication, deleteJobApplication, moveJobApplication. Order-math, tag splitting, revalidatePath. Wired into UI components.

title: Frontend: Career Plan Draggable Timeline UI
description:
Build the draggable timeline UI for the Career Plan Builder.

Using a library like `@dnd-kit/core`, create a timeline component where users can add, reorder, and edit milestones and goals. The UI should be intuitive, with clear visual cues for dragging and dropping.
close_reason: Implemented: MilestoneTimeline with @dnd-kit (DndContext, SortableContext), SortableMilestoneCard, CreateMilestoneDialog, EditMilestoneDialog. Add/reorder/edit milestones, resources per milestone. KeyboardSensor, 'Drag to reorder' hint, empty-state CTA. Server actions: createMilestone, updateMilestone, deleteMilestone, reorderMilestones.

title: Backend: Career Plan CRUD APIs
description:
Implement API endpoints for Career Plan CRUD operations.

Create API routes (`/api/plans`) to handle Create, Read, Update, and Delete operations for career plans and their associated goals. Use Drizzle ORM to interact with the `career_plans` and `goals` tables. Ensure all operations are authenticated and authorized.

title: Frontend: Connect Career Plan UI to Backend
description:
Connect the Career Plan UI to the backend APIs.

Use React Query's `useQuery` to fetch the user's career plan and `useMutation` to handle create, update, and delete actions. Implement optimistic updates for a smooth user experience when reordering or editing items.

title: Feature: Printable/Shareable Career Plans
description:
Implement printable/shareable format generation for career plans.

Create an API route that generates a public, read-only version of a career plan. For printing, create a print-specific CSS stylesheet to format the plan page cleanly. A library like `react-to-print` could be used for the frontend.

title: DevOps: Configure Monitoring and Logging
description:
Set up monitoring and logging with Axiom, PostHog, and Sentry.

Integrate the Axiom and Sentry SDKs into the Next.js app for logging and error tracking. Add the PostHog script for product analytics. Configure environment variables for all three services.

title: DevOps: Configure Global Replicas
description:
Configure global replicas for Salesforce and Supabase.

For Supabase, enable read replicas in different geographical regions via the Supabase dashboard. For Salesforce, configure the integration to connect to the appropriate regional endpoint. This is crucial for global scalability and low latency.

title: DevOps: Configure Cloudflare CDN
description:
Configure Cloudflare CDN for Vercel deployments.

In the Vercel project settings, enable the Cloudflare CDN integration. Configure caching rules for static assets to ensure optimal performance and offload traffic from the origin servers.

title: Security: PII Anonymization Pipeline
description:
Implement strict PII leakage prevention pipeline.

Create a utility service that uses a library like `pii-filter` or a custom regex-based approach to scan all user-generated text (transcripts, goals) before it's sent to third-party AI models. This should strip names, emails, phone numbers, etc.

title: Frontend: Learner Dashboard UI
description:
Create the Learner/Job Seeker dashboard.

Build the main dashboard page (`/dashboard`) for the 'learner' role. It should provide an overview of their progress, links to retake interviews, a summary of their career plan, and their current archetype.

title: Frontend: Mentor Dashboard UI (Initial)
description:
Create the initial Mentor/Coach dashboard structure.

Build the main dashboard page (`/dashboard/mentor`) for the 'mentor' role. Initially, this will be a placeholder, but it should be protected by RBAC. It will later contain lists of assigned learners and review tools.

title: JT6: Drag & Drop Architecture with dnd-kit
description:
Implement the complex column-and-card drag-and-drop mechanics shown in the tutorial (Video 4:39:00-5:23:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT6.1] Install Dependencies: npm install @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/utilities @dnd-kit/sortable
[JT6.2] Context Providers: Wrap the Kanban grid in DndContext and SortableContext. Configure closestCorners collision detection and PointerSensor with an 8px activation constraint.
[JT6.3] Sortable Hooks: Implement useDroppable on the column containers and useSortable on the Job Cards. Apply CSS transform utilities for smooth drag animations.
[JT6.4] Active States & Overlays: Create the activeId state. Render a DragOverlay component with 50% opacity to prevent layout shifting during movement.
[JT6.5] Drag End Algorithm (Critical): Implement handleDragEnd. Calculate target vs source indices. Apply the mathematical algorithm (multiply index by 100 to leave spacing) to determine the new order integer without rewriting the entire array.
[JT6.6] Optimistic UI & Persist: Update the local React state immediately on drop, then trigger the updateJobApplication Server Action in the background to persist the new columnId and order to PostgreSQL.

PACKAGE DEPS: @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/utilities, @dnd-kit/sortable
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-5 (Kanban UI), deeppivot-6 (Server Actions for persistence)
close_reason: Full dnd-kit integration: DndContext + SortableContext + DroppableColumn + SortableJobCard + DragOverlay. Optimistic UI with server-state sync. Order-math midpoint algorithm for insertions. Cross-column drag via handleDragOver, same-column reorder via arrayMove.

title: Frontend: WDB Partner Dashboard UI (Initial)
description:
Create the initial WDB Partner dashboard structure.

Build the main dashboard page (`/dashboard/wdb`) for the 'wdb_partner' role. This will be a placeholder for future data visualizations and referral management tools. Protect it with RBAC.

title: Feature: First-Time User Onboarding Flow
description:
Implement the 'First-Time User' onboarding flow.

Guide new users through a sequence of steps after their first login: 1. A prompt to take their first interview. 2. Display their archetype after the interview. 3. Guide them to the career plan builder. 4. Show links to WDB/mentor resources.

title: Feature: Returning User Dashboard Flow
description:
Implement the 'Returning User' dashboard flow.

For users who have completed the onboarding, the dashboard should present their existing data. This includes a list of past interviews, their current career plan progress, and options to share/export their plan.

title: Frontend: Landing Page and CTA
description:
Create a generic landing page with a clear Call-to-Action (CTA).

Design and build a public-facing landing page (`/`) that explains the platform's value proposition. It should feature a prominent CTA button that directs users to the registration page (`/signup`).

title: Backend: Emotion Inference Fallback Logic
description:
Implement fallback for emotion inference failure.

In the interview processing logic, add error handling for the Hume.ai integration. If the API call fails or returns an error, the system should gracefully degrade and proceed with feedback generation using only the transcript text. The feedback UI should indicate that emotion analysis is unavailable.

title: Backend: Database Connection Pooling
description:
Set up database connection pooling.

Configure the Drizzle/node-postgres client to use connection pooling to handle concurrent database requests efficiently. Set appropriate pool size limits based on the Neon database plan.

title: DB: Seed Archetypes Table
description:
Seed the database with initial archetypes.

Create a seed script that populates the `archetypes` table with a predefined set of career archetypes (e.g., 'The Analyst', 'The Creator', 'The Leader'), each with a name and a base description.

title: DB: Seed Agent Configurations
description:
Seed the database with initial agent configurations.

Create a seed script to populate the `agent_config` table with a few default interview types (e.g., 'Behavioral Interview', 'Technical Screening'). Each config should have a name, a system prompt for the LLM, and a default voice ID from ElevenLabs/PlayHT.

title: DB: Create and Populate Alt-Ed Programs Table
description:
Create a database of Alt-Ed bootcamps and certifications.

Create a new table `alt_ed_programs` (id, name, provider, cost, duration, description). Write a script to scrape or manually populate this table with data for at least 500 programs.

title: Frontend: Alt-Ed Explorer UI
description:
Build the UI for the Alt-Ed Explorer.

Create a page (`/explorer/alt-ed`) with a searchable and filterable list of the programs from the `alt_ed_programs` table. Use Shadcn components for filters (checkboxes, sliders for cost/duration) and a card layout to display the programs.

title: JT7: End-to-End Testing & Synergy Review
description:
Finalize the integration and ensure it works perfectly within the Epicflow production environment.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT7.1] Workflow Trigger Testing: Create a test workflow in Epicflow ('Webhook Trigger' -> 'Add to Job Board'). Fire the webhook and confirm the job appears in the Kanban board immediately upon refresh.
[JT7.2] UI Synergy Check: Ensure the kanban board's dark mode styling matches the global CSS variables defined in Epicflow Bead 02 theme system. Verify all Shadcn components respect the theme.
[JT7.3] Build Verification: Run npm run build to catch any lingering TypeScript errors inherited from the complex dnd-kit event types.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] User sign-up successfully creates the initial Prisma records (board + 5 columns)
- [ ] The Epicflow canvas allows dropping the Job Tracker node
- [ ] Workflows successfully execute and create JobApplications
- [ ] Drag-and-drop kanban mechanics function smoothly with optimistic UI updates
- [ ] Dark mode styling is consistent with Epicflow theme

DEPENDS ON: All previous JT issues (deeppivot-1 through deeppivot-7)
close_reason: Build passes clean (fixed pre-existing MicFFT AutoSizer type issue). Dark mode audit: all components use Shadcn semantic tokens. Workflow pipeline verified: Clerk webhook -> user creation -> board init -> node executor -> kanban display. All 8 beads (JT1-JT7) complete.

title: Backend: API for Alt-Ed Explorer
description:
Implement API and data fetching for Alt-Ed Explorer.

Create an API route that accepts filter and search parameters, queries the `alt_ed_programs` table, and returns a paginated list of results. Use React Query on the frontend to fetch and cache this data.

title: Feature: Alt-Ed ROI Analysis
description:
Implement ROI analysis for Alt-Ed programs.

For each program in the Alt-Ed Explorer, add a feature to calculate a simple ROI. This could be based on user-provided current salary vs. post-program expected salary (from industry data) against the program's cost and duration.

title: Feature: Grant/Funding Eligibility Sync
description:
Implement grant/funding eligibility logic.

Add logic to the Alt-Ed Explorer that cross-references programs with known grant or funding sources (e.g., WIOA-approved programs). This could be a simple tag or a more complex check against user profile data.

title: Backend: Salesforce Integration Setup
description:
Set up Salesforce integration credentials and API client.

Obtain Salesforce API credentials for the WDB partner instance. Set up an API client using a library like `jsforce`. Store credentials securely in environment variables. Create a service file (`lib/salesforce.ts`) to encapsulate API calls.

title: Backend: Salesforce Data Sync Job
description:
Develop Inngest job for syncing WDB data from Salesforce.

Create a scheduled Inngest job that runs periodically (e.g., daily). This job will connect to Salesforce, fetch relevant data (e.g., WDB client information), and sync it with the application's database, potentially updating `user_profiles` or a dedicated WDB table.

title: Feature: Sync Career Plan with WDB Data
description:
Integrate WDB data into the Career Plan Builder.

Allow career plans to be linked to a user's WDB record. When a user is identified as a WDB client (via Salesforce sync), provide options in the career plan builder to align goals with their official WDB plan.

title: Feature: Mentor Video Review Tool
description:
Implement mentor tool to review learner's video sessions.

On the mentor dashboard, create a UI for mentors to view a list of their assigned learners. Allow them to click on a learner to see their past interview recordings, transcripts, and the AI-generated feedback.

title: Feature: Mentor Feedback/Commenting
description:
Implement mentor tool to add feedback to sessions.

Add a feature allowing mentors to add their own comments or feedback on top of the AI-generated feedback for a learner's interview. This feedback should be stored in a new table, e.g., `mentor_feedback`.

title: Feature: Mentor Referral Pathways
description:
Implement referral pathways for mentors.

Create a feature on the mentor dashboard that allows a mentor to generate a referral for a learner. This could be a referral to a WDB partner, a specific job opening, or another resource. The referral action should be logged.

title: Feature: Mentor Resource Sharing
description:
Implement resource sharing for mentors.

Allow mentors to curate a list of resources (links, documents) and share them with their learners. This could be a simple text field on the mentor dashboard or a more structured resource management system.

title: LP1: Dashboard Shell & Sidebar Navigation
description:
Create a shared /dashboard layout with sidebar navigation that connects all feature pages. Currently the only dashboard route is /dashboard/job-tracker with no layout wrapper — users have no way to navigate between features.

SUB-ISSUES:
[LP1.1] Create app/dashboard/layout.tsx with a collapsible sidebar (desktop: fixed sidebar, mobile: sheet/drawer).
[LP1.2] Sidebar links: Interviews, Analytics, Career Archetype, Career Plan, Mentors, Education Explorer, Job Tracker. Each with icon and label.
[LP1.3] User profile section at bottom of sidebar (Clerk UserButton + user name).
[LP1.4] Wire existing /dashboard/job-tracker into the new layout.
[LP1.5] Create /dashboard/page.tsx as a hub/overview page with cards linking to each feature.

KEY FILES: app/dashboard/layout.tsx, app/dashboard/page.tsx
SHADCN DEPS: May need sheet, sidebar, or similar components.
DEPENDS ON: Nothing — this is the foundation for all other LP beads.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: WDB Partner Dashboard Visualizations
description:
Build out the WDB Partner dashboard with data visualizations.

Enhance the WDB Partner dashboard to show key metrics and visualizations about their client cohort. This could include charts showing interview completion rates, common archetypes, and progress on career plans.

title: AI/ML: Archetype Model Bias Detection
description:
Implement model bias detection checks for archetyping.

Develop a post-processing step for the archetyping engine. This step will analyze the distribution of archetypes across demographic segments (if available and ethically permissible) to check for statistical bias. Flag significant imbalances for human review.

title: Admin: Human Review Queue for Archetypes
description:
Create a human review queue for flagged archetypes.

Build a simple admin interface where flagged archetyping results can be reviewed by a human. The reviewer should be able to see the input data, the AI's output, and either approve or override the generated archetype.

title: Performance: WebRTC Latency Tuning
description:
Optimize WebRTC settings for latency.

Fine-tune the WebRTC configuration used by the Stream SDK. This includes experimenting with different STUN/TURN server configurations, adjusting codec preferences, and potentially implementing dynamic bitrate adjustments to maintain low latency even on weaker networks.

title: Auth: RBAC for Enterprise Role
description:
Refine RBAC for Enterprise Talent Manager role.

Define and implement the specific permissions for the 'enterprise_manager' role. This role should have read-only access to a cohort of users within their organization but no access to other users' data.

title: AI/ML: Adaptive Feedback Engine
description:
Implement adaptive feedback based on user history.

Enhance the feedback generation prompt. Before calling the LLM, fetch the user's past interview feedback. Instruct the LLM to consider previous feedback and tailor the new feedback to highlight improvement or recurring issues.

title: AI/ML: Predictive Career Analytics
description:
Develop predictive career analytics.

Create a service that analyzes a user's combined data (archetype, skills, plan goals, interview performance). Use an LLM to generate predictive insights, such as 'Recommended next skill to learn' or 'Potential career paths to explore'.

title: DevEx: Component Storybook
description:
Create a style guide and component storybook.

Use a tool like Storybook to document all Shadcn and custom components. For each component, document its props, variants, and usage examples. This ensures consistency as the team grows.

title: Frontend: Responsive App Layout
description:
Create a responsive layout shell.

Implement the main application layout component, including a responsive sidebar/navigation and a main content area. Ensure the layout adapts gracefully to mobile, tablet, and desktop screen sizes using Tailwind's responsive breakpoints.

title: Auth: Forgot Password Flow
description:
Implement 'Forgot Password' functionality.

Create a flow for password recovery. A user enters their email, the system generates a secure, time-limited token, and sends a password reset link via email. The user clicks the link to set a new password.
